samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>None.</parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="a_prev">Previous value of input signal.</reg>
        <reg id="rise_delay">Delay for rising edge detection.</reg>
        <reg id="down_delay">Delay for falling edge detection.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    <sequential_logic>
        <always_comb id="a_prev">
            <behavior>
                At the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, if reset is active, the previous value of the input signal a is set to 0. Otherwise, it is updated to the current value of a.
            </behavior>
        </always_comb>
        <always_comb id="rise_delay">
            <behavior>
                At the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, if reset is active, the delay for the rising edge detection is set to 0. Otherwise, it is updated to the logical OR of the condition where the previous value of a is 0 and the current value of a is 1.
            </behavior>
        </always_comb>
        <always_comb id="down_delay">
            <behavior>
                At the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, if reset is active, the delay for the falling edge detection is set to 0. Otherwise, it is updated to the logical OR of the condition where the previous value of a is 1 and the current value of a is 0.
            </behavior>
        </always_comb>
    </sequential_logic>
    <sequential_logic>
        <always_comb id="rise">
            <behavior>
                At the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, if reset is active, the rise output is set to 0. If the delay for rising edge detection is 1, the rise output is set to 1. Otherwise, it is set to 0.
            </behavior>
        </always_comb>
    </sequential_logic>
    <sequential_logic>
        <always_comb id="down">
            <behavior>
                At the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of reset, if reset is active, the down output is set to 0. If the delay for falling edge detection is 1, the down output is set to 1. Otherwise, it is set to 0.
            </behavior>
        </always_comb>
    </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>
